choices.text: Single-shard high-performance L1: 3-5k TPS typical, 400ms blocks, <$0.001 fees. Trade-off: high validator hardware requirements (fewer validators) + occasional chain halts. Different from L2 scaling approach.

questions.explanation: Monolithic L1 scaling via high-performance single shard.

choices.text: Programs are stateless executables; state lives in accounts (serialized blobs at addresses); transactions must pass relevant accounts. Different from Ethereum's contracts-hold-state model. Implication: structure code around explicit account access.

questions.explanation: Stateless programs + accounts model; must pass accounts explicitly.
